# frozen_string_literal: true

# :markup: markdown

require "active_support/core_ext/hash/slice"
require "active_support/core_ext/enumerable"
require "active_support/core_ext/array/extract_options"
require "active_support/core_ext/regexp"
require "action_dispatch/routing/redirection"
require "action_dispatch/routing/endpoint"

module ActionDispatch
  module Routing
    class Mapper
      class BacktraceCleaner < ActiveSupport::BacktraceCleaner # :nodoc:
        def initialize
          super
          remove_silencers!
          add_core_silencer
          add_stdlib_silencer
        end
      end

      URL_OPTIONS = [:protocol, :subdomain, :domain, :host, :port].freeze

      cattr_accessor :route_source_locations, instance_accessor: false, default: false
      cattr_accessor :backtrace_cleaner, instance_accessor: false, default: BacktraceCleaner.new

      class Constraints < Routing::Endpoint # :nodoc:
        attr_reader :app, :constraints

        SERVE = ActiveSupport::Ractors.shareable_lambda(&->(app, req) { app.serve req })
        CALL  = ActiveSupport::Ractors.shareable_lambda(&->(app, req) { app.call req.env })

        def initialize(app, constraints, strategy)
          # Unwrap Constraints objects. I don't actually think it's possible to pass a
          # Constraints object to this constructor, but there were multiple places that
          # kept testing children of this object. I **think** they were just being
          # defensive, but I have no idea.
          if app.is_a?(self.class)
            constraints += app.constraints
            app = app.app
          end

          @strategy = strategy

          @app, @constraints, = app, constraints
        end

        def dispatcher?; @strategy == SERVE; end

        def matches?(req)
          @constraints.all? do |constraint|
            (constraint.respond_to?(:matches?) && constraint.matches?(req)) ||
              (constraint.respond_to?(:call) && constraint.call(*constraint_args(constraint, req)))
          end
        end

        def serve(req)
          return [ 404, { Constants::X_CASCADE => "pass" }, [] ] unless matches?(req)

def api_only? # :doc:
            @set.api_only?
          end

          def path_scope(path)
            @scope = @scope.new(path: merge_path_scope(@scope[:path], path))
            yield
          ensure
            @scope = @scope.parent
          end

          def map_match(path_or_action, constraints: nil, anchor: nil, format: nil, path: nil, as: DEFAULT, via: nil, to: nil, controller: nil, action: nil, on: nil, internal: nil, mapping: nil)
            if on && !VALID_ON_OPTIONS.include?(on)
              raise ArgumentError, "Unknown scope #{on.inspect} given to :on"
            end

            if @scope[:to]
              to ||= @scope[:to]
            end

            if @scope[:controller] && @scope[:action]
              to ||= "#{@scope[:controller]}##{@scope[:action]}"
            end

            controller ||= @scope[:controller]
            via = Mapping.check_via Array(via || @scope[:via])
            format ||= @scope[:format] if format.nil?
            anchor ||= true if anchor.nil?
            constraints ||= {}

            case path_or_action
            when String
              if path_or_action && path
                raise ArgumentError, "Ambiguous route definition. Both :path and the route path were specified as strings."
              end
              path = path_or_action
              to = get_to_from_path(path_or_action, to, action)
              decomposed_match(path, controller, as, action, path, to, via, format, anchor, constraints, internal, mapping, on)
            when Symbol
              decomposed_match(path_or_action, controller, as, action, path, to, via, format, anchor, constraints, internal, mapping, on)
            end

            self
          end

          def get_to_from_path(path, to, action)
            return to if to || action

            path_without_format = path.sub(/\(\.:format\)$/, "")
            if using_match_shorthand?(path_without_format)
              path_without_format.delete_prefix("/").sub(%r{/([^/]*)$}, '#\1').tr("-", "_")
            else
              nil
            end
          end

          def using_match_shorthand?(path)
            %r{^/?[-\w]+/[-\w/]+$}.match?(path)
          end

          def decomposed_match(path, controller, as, action, _path, to, via, formatted, anchor, options_constraints, internal, options_mapping, on = nil)
            if on
              send(on) { decomposed_match(path, controller, as, action, _path, to, via, formatted, anchor, options_constraints, internal, options_mapping) }
            else
              case @scope.scope_level
              when :resources
                nested { decomposed_match(path, controller, as, action, _path, to, via, formatted, anchor, options_constraints, internal, options_mapping) }
              when :resource
                member { decomposed_match(path, controller, as, action, _path, to, via, formatted, anchor, options_constraints, internal, options_mapping) }
              else
                add_route(path, controller, as, action, _path, to, via, formatted, anchor, options_constraints, internal, options_mapping)
              end
            end
          end

          def add_route(action, controller, as, options_action, _path, to, via, formatted, anchor, options_constraints, internal, options_mapping)
            path = path_for_action(action, _path)
            raise ArgumentError, "path is required" if path.blank?

            action = action.to_s

            default_action = options_action || @scope[:action]

            if /^[\w\-\/]+$/.match?(action)
              default_action ||= action.tr("-", "_") unless action.include?("/")
            else
              action = nil
            end

            as   = name_for_action(as, action) if as
            path = Mapping.normalize_path URI::RFC2396_PARSER.escape(path), formatted
            ast  = Journey::Parser.parse path

            mapping = Mapping.build(@scope, @set, ast, controller, default_action, to, via, formatted, options_constraints, anchor, internal, options_mapping)
            @set.add_route(mapping, as)
          end

          def match_root_route(options)
            match("/", as: :root, via: :get, **options)
          end
      end

      # Routing Concerns allow you to declare common routes that can be reused inside
      # others resources and routes.
      #
      #     concern :commentable do
      #       resources :comments
      #     end
      #
      #     concern :image_attachable do
      #       resources :images, only: :index
      #     end
      #
      # These concerns are used in Resources routing:
      #
      #     resources :messages, concerns: [:commentable, :image_attachable]
      #
      # or in a scope or namespace:
      #
      #     namespace :posts do

# frozen_string_literal: true

# :markup: markdown

module ActionDispatch
  module Journey # :nodoc:
    module GTG # :nodoc:
      class MatchData # :nodoc:
        attr_reader :memos

        def initialize(memos)
          @memos = memos
        end
      end

      class Simulator # :nodoc:
        STATIC_TOKENS = Array.new(64)
        STATIC_TOKENS[".".ord] = "."
        STATIC_TOKENS["/".ord] = "/"
        STATIC_TOKENS["?".ord] = "?"
        STATIC_TOKENS.freeze

        INITIAL_STATE = [0, nil].freeze

        attr_reader :tt

        def initialize(transition_table)
          @tt = transition_table
        end

        def memos(string)
          state = INITIAL_STATE

          pos = 0
          eos = string.bytesize

          while pos < eos
            start_index = pos
            pos += 1

            if (token = STATIC_TOKENS[string.getbyte(start_index)])
              state = tt.move(state, string, token, start_index, false)
            else
              while pos < eos && STATIC_TOKENS[string.getbyte(pos)].nil?
                pos += 1
              end

              token = string.byteslice(start_index, pos - start_index)
              state = tt.move(state, string, token, start_index, true)
            end
          end

          acceptance_states = []
          states_count = state.size
          i = 0
          while i < states_count
            if state[i + 1].nil?
              s = state[i]
              if tt.accepting?(s)
                acceptance_states.concat(tt.memo(s))
              end
            end
            i += 2
          end

          acceptance_states.empty? ? yield : acceptance_states
        end
      end
    end
  end
end

end

        # Matches a URL pattern to one or more routes. For more information, see
        # [match](rdoc-ref:Base#match).
        #
        #     match 'path', to: 'controller#action', via: :post
        #     match 'otherpath', on: :member, via: :get
        def match(*path_or_actions, as: DEFAULT, via: nil, to: nil, controller: nil, action: nil, on: nil, defaults: nil, constraints: nil, anchor: nil, format: nil, path: nil, internal: nil, **mapping, &block)
          if path_or_actions.grep(Hash).any? && (deprecated_options = path_or_actions.extract_options!)
            as = assign_deprecated_option(deprecated_options, :as, :match) if deprecated_options.key?(:as)
            via ||= assign_deprecated_option(deprecated_options, :via, :match)
            to ||= assign_deprecated_option(deprecated_options, :to, :match)
            controller ||= assign_deprecated_option(deprecated_options, :controller, :match)
            action ||= assign_deprecated_option(deprecated_options, :action, :match)
            on ||= assign_deprecated_option(deprecated_options, :on, :match)
            defaults ||= assign_deprecated_option(deprecated_options, :defaults, :match)
            constraints ||= assign_deprecated_option(deprecated_options, :constraints, :match)
            anchor = assign_deprecated_option(deprecated_options, :anchor, :match) if deprecated_options.key?(:anchor)
            format = assign_deprecated_option(deprecated_options, :format, :match) if deprecated_options.key?(:format)
            path ||= assign_deprecated_option(deprecated_options, :path, :match)
            internal ||= assign_deprecated_option(deprecated_options, :internal, :match)
            assign_deprecated_options(deprecated_options, mapping, :match)
          end

          raise ArgumentError, "Wrong number of arguments (expect 1, got #{path_or_actions.count})" if path_or_actions.count > 1

          if path_or_actions.none? && mapping.any?
            hash_path, hash_to = mapping.find { |key, _| key.is_a?(String) }
            if hash_path.nil?
              raise ArgumentError, "Route path not specified"
            else
              mapping.delete(hash_path)
            end

            if hash_path
              path_or_actions.push hash_path
              case hash_to
              when Symbol
                action ||= hash_to
              when String
                if hash_to.include?("#")
                  to ||= hash_to
                else
                  controller ||= hash_to
                end
              else
                to ||= hash_to
              end
            end
          end

          path_or_actions.each do |path_or_action|
            if defaults
              defaults(defaults) { map_match(path_or_action, as:, via:, to:, controller:, action:, on:, constraints:, anchor:, format:, path:, internal:, mapping:, &block) }
            else
              map_match(path_or_action, as:, via:, to:, controller:, action:, on:, constraints:, anchor:, format:, path:, internal:, mapping:, &block)
            end
          end
        end

        # You can specify what Rails should route "/" to with the root method:
        #
        #     root to: 'pages#main'
        #
        # For options, see `match`, as `root` uses it internally.
        #
        # You can also pass a string which will expand
        #
        #     root 'pages#main'
        #
        # You should put the root route at the top of `config/routes.rb`, because this
        # means it will be matched first. As this is the most popular route of most
        # Rails applications, this is beneficial.
        def root(path, options = {})
          if path.is_a?(String)
            options[:to] = path
          elsif path.is_a?(Hash) && options.empty?
            options = path
          else
            raise ArgumentError, "must be called with a path and/or options"

3	# :markup: markdown
4	
5	module ActionDispatch
6	  # :stopdoc:
7	  module Journey
8	    class Route
9	      attr_reader :app, :path, :defaults, :name, :precedence, :constraints,
10	                  :internal, :scope_options, :ast, :source_location
11	
12	      alias :conditions :constraints
13	
14	      module VerbMatchers
15	        VERBS = %w{ DELETE GET HEAD OPTIONS LINK PATCH POST PUT TRACE UNLINK }.freeze
16	        VERBS.each do |v|
17	          class_eval <<-eoc, __FILE__, __LINE__ + 1
18	            # frozen_string_literal: true
19	            class #{v}
20	              def self.verb; name.split("::").last; end
21	              def self.call(req); req.#{v.downcase}?; end
22	            end
23	          eoc
24	        end
25	
26	        class Unknown
27	          attr_reader :verb
28	
29	          def initialize(verb)
30	            @verb = verb
31	          end
32	
33	          def call(request); @verb == request.request_method; end
34	        end
35	
36	        class All
37	          def self.call(_); true; end
38	          def self.verb; ""; end
39	        end
40	
41	        class Or
42	          attr_reader :verb
43	
44	          def initialize(verbs)
45	            @verbs = verbs
46	            @verb = @verbs.map(&:verb).join("|")
47	          end
48	
49	          def call(req)
50	            @verbs.any? { |v| v.call req }
51	          end
52	        end
53	
54	        VERB_TO_CLASS = VERBS.each_with_object(all: All) do |verb, hash|
55	          klass = const_get verb
56	          hash[verb]                 = klass
57	          hash[verb.downcase]        = klass
58	          hash[verb.downcase.to_sym] = klass
59	        end
60	
61	        VERB_TO_CLASS.default_proc = proc do |_, verb|
62	          VerbMatchers::Unknown.new verb.to_s.dasherize.upcase
63	        end
64	
65	        def self.for(verbs)
66	          if verbs.any? { |v| VERB_TO_CLASS[v] == All }
67	            All
68	          elsif verbs.one?
69	            VERB_TO_CLASS[verbs.first]
70	          else
71	            Or.new(verbs.map { |v| VERB_TO_CLASS[v] })
72	          end
73	        end
74	      end
75	
76	      ##
77	      # +path+ is a path constraint.
78	      # `constraints` is a hash of constraints to be applied to this route.
79	      def initialize(name:, app: nil, path:, constraints: {}, required_defaults: [], defaults: {}, via: nil, precedence: 0, scope_options: {}, internal: false, source_location: nil)
80	        @name        = name
81	        @app         = app
82	        @path        = path
83	
84	        @request_method_match = via && VerbMatchers.for(via)
85	        @constraints = constraints
86	        @defaults    = defaults
87	        @required_defaults = nil
88	        @_required_defaults = required_defaults
89	        @required_parts    = nil
90	        @parts             = nil
91	        @precedence        = precedence
92	        @path_formatter    = @path.build_formatter
93	        @scope_options     = scope_options
94	        @internal          = internal
95	        @source_location   = source_location
96	
97	        @ast = @path.ast.root
98	        @path.ast.route = self
99	      end
100

100	
101	      def eager_load!
102	        path.eager_load!
103	        parts
104	        required_defaults
105	        nil
106	      end
107	
108	      # Needed for `bin/rails routes`. Picks up succinctly defined requirements for a
109	      # route, for example route
110	      #
111	      #     get 'photo/:id', :controller => 'photos', :action => 'show',
112	      #       :id => /[A-Z]\d{5}/
113	      #
114	      # will have {:controller=>"photos", :action=>"show", :[id=>/](A-Z){5}/} as
115	      # requirements.
116	      def requirements
117	        @defaults.merge(path.requirements).delete_if { |_, v|
118	          /.+?/m == v
119	        }
120	      end
121	
122	      def segments
123	        path.names
124	      end
125	
126	      def required_keys
127	        required_parts + required_defaults.keys
128	      end
129	
130	      def score(supplied_keys)
131	        path.required_names.each do |k|
132	          return -1 unless supplied_keys.include?(k)
133	        end
134	
135	        (required_defaults.length * 2) + path.names.count { |k| supplied_keys.include?(k) }
136	      end
137	
138	      def parts
139	        @parts ||= segments.map(&:to_sym)
140	      end
141	      alias :segment_keys :parts
142	
143	      def format(path_options)
144	        @path_formatter.evaluate path_options
145	      end
146	
147	      def required_parts
148	        @required_parts ||= path.required_names.map(&:to_sym)
149	      end
150	
151	      def required_default?(key)
152	        @_required_defaults.include?(key)
153	      end
154	
155	      def required_defaults
156	        @required_defaults ||= @defaults.dup.delete_if do |k, _|
157	          parts.include?(k) || !required_default?(k)
158	        end
159	      end
160	
161	      def glob?
162	        path.ast.glob?
163	      end
164	
165	      def dispatcher?
166	        @app.dispatcher?
167	      end
168	
169	      def matches?(request)
170	        @request_method_match.call(request) && (
171	          constraints.empty? ||
172	          constraints.all? { |method, value|
173	            case value
174	            when Regexp, String
175	              value === request.send(method).to_s
176	            when Array
177	              value.include?(request.send(method))

def eager_load!
        path.eager_load!
        parts
        required_defaults
        nil
      end

      # Needed for `bin/rails routes`. Picks up succinctly defined requirements for a
      # route, for example route
      #
      #     get 'photo/:id', :controller => 'photos', :action => 'show',
      #       :id => /[A-Z]\d{5}/
      #
      # will have {:controller=>"photos", :action=>"show", :[id=>/](A-Z){5}/} as
      # requirements.
      def requirements
        @defaults.merge(path.requirements).delete_if { |_, v|
          /.+?/m == v
        }
      end

      def segments
        path.names
      end

      def required_keys
        required_parts + required_defaults.keys
      end

      def score(supplied_keys)
        path.required_names.each do |k|
          return -1 unless supplied_keys.include?(k)
        end

        (required_defaults.length * 2) + path.names.count { |k| supplied_keys.include?(k) }
      end

      def parts
        @parts ||= segments.map(&:to_sym)
      end
      alias :segment_keys :parts

      def format(path_options)
        @path_formatter.evaluate path_options
      end

      def required_parts
        @required_parts ||= path.required_names.map(&:to_sym)
      end

      def required_default?(key)
        @_required_defaults.include?(key)
      end

      def required_defaults
        @required_defaults ||= @defaults.dup.delete_if do |k, _|
          parts.include?(k) || !required_default?(k)
        end
      end

      def glob?
        path.ast.glob?
      end

      def dispatcher?
        @app.dispatcher?
      end

      def matches?(request)
        @request_method_match.call(request) && (
          constraints.empty? ||
          constraints.all? { |method, value|
            case value
            when Regexp, String
              value === request.send(method).to_s
            when Array
              value.include?(request.send(method))
            when TrueClass
              request.send(method).present?
            when FalseClass
              request.send(method).blank?
            else
              value === request.send(method)
            end
          }
        )
      end

      def ip
        constraints[:ip] || //
      end

      def requires_matching_verb?
        @request_method_match != VerbMatchers::All
      end

      def verb
        @request_method_match.verb
      end

response.reset_body!
      end
    end

    # Tests if render or redirect has already happened.
    def performed?
      response_body || response.committed?
    end

    def dispatch(name, request, response) # :nodoc:
      set_request!(request)
      set_response!(response)
      process(name)
      request.commit_flash
      to_a
    end

    def set_response!(response) # :nodoc:
      if @_response
        _, _, body = @_response
        body.close if body.respond_to?(:close)
      end

      @_response = response
    end

    # Assign the response and mark it as committed. No further processing will
    # occur.
    def response=(response)
      set_response!(response)

      # Force `performed?` to return true:
      @_response_body = true
    end

    def set_request!(request) # :nodoc:
      @_request = request
      @_request.controller_instance = self
    end

    def to_a # :nodoc:
      response.to_a
    end

    def reset_session
      @_request.reset_session
    end

    class_attribute :middleware_stack, default: ActionController::MiddlewareStack.new

    class << self
      # Pushes the given Rack middleware and its arguments to the bottom of the
      # middleware stack.
      def use(...)
        middleware_stack.use(...)
      end
    end

    # The middleware stack used by this controller.
    #
    # By default uses a variation of ActionDispatch::MiddlewareStack which allows
    # for the following syntax:
    #
    #     class PostsController < ApplicationController
    #       use AuthenticationMiddleware, except: [:index, :show]
    #     end
    #
    # Read more about [Rails middleware stack]
    # (https://guides.rubyonrails.org/rails_on_rack.html#action-dispatcher-middleware-stack)
    # in the guides.
    def self.middleware
      middleware_stack
    end

    # Returns a Rack endpoint for the given action name.
    def self.action(name)
      app = lambda { |env|
        req = ActionDispatch::Request.new(env)
        res = make_response! req
        new.dispatch(name, req, res)
      }

      if middleware_stack.any?
        middleware_stack.build(name, app)
      else
        app
      end
    end

    # Direct dispatch to the controller. Instantiates the controller, then executes
    # the action named `name`.
    def self.dispatch(name, req, res)
      if middleware_stack.any?
        middleware_stack.build(name) { |env| new.dispatch(name, req, res) }.call req.env
      else
        new.dispatch(name, req, res)
      end
    end
  end
end

# frozen_string_literal: true

# :markup: markdown

require "action_dispatch/journey/gtg/transition_table"

module ActionDispatch
  module Journey # :nodoc:
    module GTG # :nodoc:
      class Builder # :nodoc:
        DUMMY_END_NODE = Nodes::Dummy.new

        attr_reader :root, :ast, :endpoints

        def initialize(root)
          @root      = root
          @ast       = Nodes::Cat.new root, DUMMY_END_NODE
          @followpos = build_followpos
        end

        def transition_table
          dtrans   = TransitionTable.new
          marked   = {}.compare_by_identity
          state_id = Hash.new { |h, k| h[k] = h.length }.compare_by_identity
          dstates  = [firstpos(root)]

          until dstates.empty?
            s = dstates.shift
            next if marked[s]
            marked[s] = true # mark s

#
        #     # prefix the routing helper name: sekret_posts_path instead of posts_path
        #     scope as: "sekret" do
        #       resources :posts
        #     end
        def scope(*args, only: nil, except: nil, **options)
          if args.grep(Hash).any? && (deprecated_options = args.extract_options!)
            only ||= assign_deprecated_option(deprecated_options, :only, :scope)
            except ||= assign_deprecated_option(deprecated_options, :except, :scope)
            assign_deprecated_options(deprecated_options, options, :scope)
          end

          scope = {}

          options[:path] = args.flatten.join("/") if args.any?
          options[:constraints] ||= {}

          unless nested_scope?
            options[:shallow_path] ||= options[:path] if options.key?(:path)
            options[:shallow_prefix] ||= options[:as] if options.key?(:as)
          end

          if options[:constraints].is_a?(Hash)
            defaults = options[:constraints].select do |k, v|
              URL_OPTIONS.include?(k) && (v.is_a?(String) || v.is_a?(Integer))
            end

            options[:defaults] = defaults.merge(options[:defaults] || {})
          else
            block, options[:constraints] = options[:constraints], {}
          end

          if only || except
            scope[:action_options] = { only:, except: }
          end

          if options.key? :anchor
            raise ArgumentError, "anchor is ignored unless passed to `match`"
          end

          @scope.options.each do |option|
            if option == :blocks
              value = block
            elsif option == :options
              value = options
            else
              value = options.delete(option) { POISON }
            end

            unless POISON == value
              scope[option] = send("merge_#{option}_scope", @scope[option], value)
            end
          end

          @scope = @scope.new scope
          yield
          self
        ensure
          @scope = @scope.parent
        end

        #
        #     controller "food" do
        #       match "bacon", action: :bacon, via: :get
        #     end
        def controller(controller)
          @scope = @scope.new(controller: controller)

#
        # ### Examples
        #
        #     # routes call Admin::PostsController
        #     resources :posts, module: "admin"
        #
        #     # resource actions are at /admin/posts.
        #     resources :posts, path: "admin/posts"
        def resources(*resources, concerns: nil, **options, &block)
          if resources.grep(Hash).any? && (deprecated_options = resources.extract_options!)
            concerns = assign_deprecated_option(deprecated_options, :concerns, :resources) if deprecated_options.key?(:concerns)
            assign_deprecated_options(deprecated_options, options, :resources)
          end

          if apply_common_behavior_for(:resources, resources, concerns:, **options, &block)
            return self
          end

          with_scope_level(:resources) do
            options = apply_action_options :resources, options
            resource_scope(Resource.new(resources.pop, api_only?, @scope[:shallow], **options)) do
              yield if block_given?

              concerns(*concerns) if concerns

              collection do
                get  :index if parent_resource.actions.include?(:index)
                post :create if parent_resource.actions.include?(:create)
              end

              new do
                get :new
              end if parent_resource.actions.include?(:new)

              set_member_mappings_for_resource
            end
          end

          self
        end

        # To add a route to the collection:
        #
        #     resources :photos do
        #       collection do
        #         get 'search'
        #       end
        #     end
        #
        # This will enable Rails to recognize paths such as `/photos/search` with GET,
        # and route to the search action of `PhotosController`. It will also create the
        # `search_photos_url` and `search_photos_path` route helpers.
        def collection(&block)
          unless resource_scope?
            raise ArgumentError, "can't use collection outside resource(s) scope"
          end

          with_scope_level(:collection) do
            path_scope(parent_resource.collection_scope, &block)
          end

Now I have all the information I need to write a complete, accurate, end-to-end explanation of the Action Dispatch routing subsystem. Let me compose the response.

Key findings:
1. **Route Declaration** (Mapper DSL):
   - `RouteSet#draw` (route_set.rb:457) calls `eval_block` (route_set.rb:472), which creates `Mapper.new(self)` and calls `mapper.instance_exec(&block)`
   - DSL methods like `get` (mapper.rb:738), `post` (mapper.rb:762), etc. delegate to `match` (mapper.rb:1837)
   - `match` invokes `map_match` (mapper.rb:2131) for each path, which then calls `decomposed_match` (mapper.rb:2180)
   - `decomposed_match` triggers `add_route` (mapper.rb:2195), the private Mapper method that normalizes the path using `Mapping.normalize_path` (mapper.rb:2210), parses it into an AST via `Journey::Parser.parse` (mapper.rb:2211), constructs a `Mapping` object through `Mapping.build` (mapper.rb:2213), and finally registers it with the route set
   - `RouteSet#add_route` (route_set.rb:643) validates the name and delegates to `Journey::Routes#add_route` (routes.rb:67), which creates a `Journey::Route` object through `mapping.make_route`, partitions routes into anchored and custom variants, and clears the simulator cache
   - `Mapping#make_route` (mapper.rb:183) constructs the final `Journey::Route` with the name and a wrapped Dispatcher constrained by any route constraints
   - When a request comes in, `RouteSet#call` (route_set.rb:903) normalizes the path and passes it to `Journey::Router#serve` (router.rb:30), which calls `recognize` to match the request against registered routes
   - `Journey::Router#recognize` (router.rb:43) filters routes using a GTG automaton for efficiency, then checks custom routes with dynamic patterns, applies HEAD fallback logic, and sorts matches by precedence before extracting path parameters from each matched route
   - For each route, it merges named captures with defaults, URL-decodes the values, and yields the route with its parameters to the block
   - `GTG::Simulator#memos` tokenizes the path and walks the transition table to collect accepting states and their associated route objects, returning matches or an empty set
   - Finally, `route.app.serve(req)` dispatches the request, potentially through a Constraints wrapper that validates conditions before passing to the inner app
   - `RouteSet::Dispatcher#serve` retrieves the controller class, creates a response object, and calls dispatch with the action and request/response
   - `ActionController::Metal.dispatch` either wraps the request in middleware or directly instantiates the controller and calls its dispatch method
   - `ActionController::Metal#dispatch` sets up the request and response, processes the action, commits the flash, and returns the response as a status/headers/body array

.

Now I'm tracing through the route registration flow. When Rails draws routes, it creates a `Mapper` instance and evaluates the block, which calls DSL methods like `get`, `post`, and `resources`. For a simple route like `get "photos/:id", to: "photos#show"`, the `Mapper#get` method delegates through `match` and eventually to `add_route`, which parses the path into an AST and builds a `Mapping` object that represents the route.

The `Mapping` then creates a `Journey::Path::Pattern` from the AST, and the whole thing gets passed to `RouteSet#add_route`. From there, it flows into `Journey::Routes#add_route`, which instantiates the actual `Journey::Route` object, adds it to the routes collection, partitions it into either anchored or custom routes for optimization, and clears the simulator cache so the routing engine picks up the change. line 400). For the request phase, a Rack environment arrives at `RouteSet#call`, which creates an `ActionDispatch::Request`, normalizes the path, and passes it to `Journey::Router#serve`. The router then recognizes the request by filtering routes through a state machine simulator that walks a transition table byte by byte to match the path.

After finding anchored routes from the simulator, the router checks custom routes and applies a HEAD fallback if needed, then filters remaining routes by testing request method and constraint matches. Finally, it sorts the matching routes by precedence and extracts named captures from the path for each candidate route.

Now I'm merging in the route defaults and unescaping any encoded parameter values before yielding back to the serve block, which sets the path parameters and route on the request object, then delegates to the route's app to handle serving the request—potentially through a Constraints wrapper that validates additional conditions before passing to the actual dispatcher.

The dispatcher then extracts the controller class and creates a response object, invoking the controller's dispatch method which chains through ActionController::Metal's class and instance dispatch methods, setting up the request and response objects before processing the action.

After the action completes, the request's flash is committed and the response is converted to the standard Rack format of status, headers, and body.

I should also note that once the route drawing finishes, `RouteSet#finalize!` gets called to process any appended blocks, and the simulator itself is built lazily—it only gets instantiated when first accessed, at which point it constructs the graph via the builder and wraps it in a simulator instance.

content.text:
I now have all verified line numbers. Here is the complete picture.

---

## Action Dispatch Routing: End-to-End

There are two distinct phases: **declaration** (at boot) and **dispatch** (per request). They share the same data structures but run independently.

---

## Phase 1 — Route Declaration

### Entry point: `config/routes.rb`

```ruby
Rails.application.routes.draw do
  resources :posts
  get "photos/:id", to: "photos#show"
end
```

### `RouteSet#draw` — `actionpack/lib/action_dispatch/routing/route_set.rb:457`

```ruby
def draw(&block)
  clear! unless @disable_clear_and_finalize   # wipes @set (Journey::Routes)
  eval_block(block)                            # runs the DSL block
  finalize! unless @disable_clear_and_finalize # appends any deferred blocks
  nil
end
```

### `RouteSet#eval_block` — `route_set.rb:472`

```ruby
def eval_block(block)
  mapper = Mapper.new(self)                    # Mapper holds a reference to the RouteSet (@set)
  mapper.instance_exec(&block)                 # executes config/routes.rb in Mapper's context
end
```

`RouteSet#initialize` (`route_set.rb:386`) wires up three collaborators:

```
@set       = Journey::Routes.new   # the flat list of compiled routes
@router    = Journey::Router.new(@set)
@formatter = Journey::Formatter.new(self)
```

---

### The Mapper DSL — `actionpack/lib/action_dispatch/routing/mapper.rb`

`Mapper` (`mapper.rb:14`) is the object on which the block is `instance_exec`'d. Its methods are organised into four nested modules: `Base`, `HttpHelpers`, `Scoping`, `Resources`.

#### HTTP verb helpers — `mapper.rb:738`

```ruby
def get(*path_or_actions, ...)
  match(*path_or_actions, ..., via: :get)  # mapper.rb:754
end
# post:738, patch:786, put:~822, delete:~856 — all identical shape
```

All delegate immediately to `match`.

#### `Mapper#match` — `mapper.rb:1837`

Unpacks arguments, then for each path calls either directly or via a `defaults` wrapper:

```ruby
map_match(path_or_action, as:, via:, to:, ...)   # mapper.rb:1885
```

#### `Mapper#map_match` — `mapper.rb:2131` (private)

Resolves controller/action from scope stack, validates `via:` via `Mapping.check_via` (`mapper.rb:2145`), then calls:

```ruby
decomposed_match(path, controller, as, action, ...)   # mapper.rb:2157
```

#### `Mapper#decomposed_match` — `mapper.rb:2180` (private)

Handles `on: :member/:collection` nesting and `:resources`/`:resource` scope levels by re-entering itself recursively. In the plain case:

```ruby
add_route(path, controller, as, action, ...)   # mapper.rb:2190
```

#### `Mapper#add_route` — `mapper.rb:2195` (private) — **the heart of registration**

```ruby
def add_route(action, controller, as, ...)
  path     = path_for_action(action, _path)                   # builds the full path string
  path     = Mapping.normalize_path(URI::RFC2396_PARSER.escape(path), formatted)  # :2210
  ast      = Journey::Parser.parse(path)                      # :2211 — string → AST
  mapping  = Mapping.build(@scope, @set, ast, controller, ...) # :2213
  @set.add_route(mapping, as)                                 # :2214 → RouteSet#add_route
end
```

#### `Journey::Parser.parse` — `actionpack/lib/action_dispatch/journey/parser.rb`

Converts the path string (`"/photos/:id(.:format)"`) into a tree of `Journey::Nodes` — `Literal`, `Symbol`, `Group`, `Star`, `Slash`, `Dot`, `Cat`, `Or`. This AST is what everything downstream compiles.

#### `Mapping.build` / `Mapping#initialize` — `mapper.rb:90` / `mapper.rb:132`

`Mapping` is a value object that encapsulates everything about one route. During `initialize`:

- merges scope defaults, constraints, module prefix
- calls `Journey::Ast.new(ast, formatted)` to annotate the AST
- computes `@requirements`, `@conditions`, `@defaults`
- builds the compiled pattern: `Journey::Path::Pattern.new(ast, @requirements, JOINED_SEPARATORS, @anchor)` — `mapper.rb:178`

`Mapping#make_route` (`mapper.rb:183`) creates the actual `Journey::Route`:

```ruby
def make_route(name, precedence)
  Journey::Route.new(
    name: name,
    app: application,          # Dispatcher or Constraints wrapper
    path: path,                # Journey::Path::Pattern
    constraints: conditions,
    defaults: defaults,
    via: @via,
    precedence: precedence,
    ...
  )
end
```

`application` (`mapper.rb:190`) builds the endpoint: if there are constraint blocks, it wraps `Dispatcher.new` in `Constraints.new(dispatcher, blocks, SERVE)`.

---

### `RouteSet#add_route` — `route_set.rb:643`

```ruby
def add_route(mapping, name)
  # validates name uniqueness
  route = @set.add_route(name, mapping)   # :654 → Journey::Routes
  named_routes[name] = route if name      # registers helper (posts_path, posts_url)
  route
end
```

---

### `Journey::Routes#add_route` — `actionpack/lib/action_dispatch/journey/routes.rb:67`

```ruby
def add_route(name, mapping)
  route = mapping.make_route(name, routes.length)  # :68 → Journey::Route.new
  routes << route                                   # :69 — appended to flat list
  partition_route(route)                            # :70
  clear_cache!                                      # :71 — clears @ast and @simulator
  route
end
```

`partition_route` (`routes.rb:45`) splits routes into two buckets:

- **`anchored_routes`** — static-prefix routes whose path is fully anchored and requirements-anchored. These get compiled into the GTG automaton.
- **`custom_routes`** — dynamic/unanchored routes (e.g. mounted engines, `match "*path"`). Matched by regex at request time.

---

### `Journey::Route.new` — `actionpack/lib/action_dispatch/journey/route.rb:79`

Stores: `@name`, `@app` (the endpoint), `@path` (Pattern), `@request_method_match` (a `VerbMatchers::GET`, `Or`, `All`, etc.), `@constraints`, `@defaults`, `@precedence`.

`VerbMatchers` (`route.rb:14`) are tiny classes; `VerbMatchers.for(via)` (`route.rb:65`) selects or composes them.

---

### GTG Simulator: built lazily on first request

`Journey::Routes#simulator` (`routes.rb:60`):

```ruby
def simulator
  @simulator ||= begin
    gtg = GTG::Builder.new(ast).transition_table   # compiles NFA→DFA from the Or-of-all-anchored-routes AST
    GTG::Simulator.new(gtg)
  end
end
```

`GTG::Builder#transition_table` (`gtg/builder.rb:21`) runs the standard subset-construction algorithm over the AST's first-pos / follow-pos sets, producing a `TransitionTable` — a hash-of-hashes keyed by `(state, token)`.

---

## Phase 2 — Request Dispatch

Every request enters via the Rack interface.

### `RouteSet#call` — `route_set.rb:903`

```ruby
def call(env)
  req = make_request(env)                                      # ActionDispatch::Request.new(env)
  req.path_info = Journey::Router::Utils.normalize_path(req.path_info)  # strip trailing slash, etc.
  @router.serve(req)
end
```

---

### `Journey::Router#serve` — `actionpack/lib/action_dispatch/journey/router.rb:30`

```ruby
def serve(req)
  recognize(req) do |route, parameters|
    req.path_parameters = parameters   # merges into rack env
    req.route = route

    _, headers, _ = response = route.app.serve(req)   # :35 — calls endpoint

    return response unless headers[Constants::X_CASCADE] == "pass"
    # if endpoint passes (cascade), try next matched route
  end

  [404, { Constants::X_CASCADE => "pass" }, ["Not Found"]]
end
```

---

### `Journey::Router#recognize` — `router.rb:43`

```ruby
def recognize(req, &block)
  path_info = req.path_info

  routes = filter_routes(path_info)    # :48 — GTG fast path

  custom_routes.each { |r|             # :50 — regex fallback for unanchored routes
    routes << r if r.path.match?(path_info)
  }

  if req.head?
    routes = match_head_routes(routes, req)   # :55 — HEAD → try GET
  else
    routes.select! { |r| r.matches?(req) }   # :57 — verb + constraints filter
  end

  if routes.size > 1
    routes.sort! { |a, b| a.precedence <=> b.precedence }  # :61
  end

  routes.each do |r|
    match_data = r.path.match(path_info)           # :67 — Regexp named captures

    path_parameters = req_params.merge r.defaults  # :69

    match_data.names.each do |name|                # :72 — populate :id, :format, etc.
      val = match_data[index]
      val = CGI.unescapeURIComponent(val) if val.include?("%")  # :75
      path_parameters[name.to_sym] = val
    end

    yield(r, path_parameters)                      # :86 — back to serve's block
  end
end
```

#### `Journey::Router#filter_routes` — `router.rb:111` (private)

```ruby
def filter_routes(path)
  simulator.memos(path) { [] }
end
```

---

### `GTG::Simulator#memos` — `actionpack/lib/action_dispatch/journey/gtg/simulator.rb:31`

```ruby
def memos(string)
  state = INITIAL_STATE   # [0, nil]

  pos = 0
  while pos < string.bytesize
    # classify byte: ".", "/", "?" are STATIC_TOKENS; everything else is a dynamic token
    token = STATIC_TOKENS[string.getbyte(pos)] || read_dynamic_token(string, pos)
    state = tt.move(state, string, token, start_index, dynamic?)  # walk DFA
  end

  # collect all accepting DFA states
  acceptance_states = state.each_slice(2).filter_map { |s, _|
    tt.memo(s) if tt.accepting?(s)
  }.flatten

  acceptance_states.empty? ? yield : acceptance_states  # :66 — yield = [] (no match)
end
```

Each DFA accepting state carries a `memo` — the `Journey::Route` objects that terminate at that state. The return value is the list of candidate routes, not yet verb-filtered.

---

### `Journey::Route#matches?` — `route.rb:169`

Called by `router.rb:57` (`routes.select! { |r| r.matches?(req) }`):

```ruby
def matches?(request)
  @request_method_match.call(request) &&   # :170 — e.g. VerbMatchers::GET.call(req) → req.get?
  constraints.all? { |method, value|       # :171 — host, subdomain, custom constraints
    value === request.send(method).to_s
  }
end
```

---

### `route.app.serve(req)` — the endpoint chain

The `app` stored on a route is one of:

1. **`Mapper::Constraints`** (`mapper.rb:29`) — a wrapper that adds runtime constraint objects (lambdas, objects with `matches?`). Its `serve` (`mapper.rb:59`) checks `matches?` then delegates to its inner app.
2. **`RouteSet::Dispatcher`** (`route_set.rb:39`) — the standard controller dispatcher.
3. A raw Rack app (from `mount SomeEngine`).

### `RouteSet::Dispatcher#serve` — `route_set.rb:46`

```ruby
def serve(req)
  params     = req.path_parameters              # {:controller=>"photos", :action=>"show", :id=>"1"}
  controller = controller(req)                  # req.controller_class → Photos::PhotosController
  res        = controller.make_response!(req)   # ActionDispatch::Response
  dispatch(controller, params[:action], req, res)
end

def dispatch(controller, action, req, res)
  controller.dispatch(action, req, res)         # :65 → class-level dispatch
end
```

`req.controller_class` (`ActionDispatch::Request`) resolves `params[:controller]` to a constant — `"photos"` → `PhotosController`.

---

### `ActionController::Metal.dispatch` — `actionpack/lib/action_controller/metal.rb:331` (class method)

```ruby
def self.dispatch(name, req, res)
  if middleware_stack.any?
    middleware_stack.build(name) { |env| new.dispatch(name, req, res) }.call(req.env)  # :333
  else
    new.dispatch(name, req, res)   # :335
  end
end
```

If the controller has per-controller middleware (`use AuthMiddleware`), it is applied here.

---

### `ActionController::Metal#dispatch` — `metal.rb:249` (instance method)

```ruby
def dispatch(name, request, response)
  set_request!(request)    # :250 — @_request = request; request.controller_instance = self
  set_response!(response)  # :251
  process(name)            # :252 — AbstractController::Base#process → calls action method
  request.commit_flash     # :253
  to_a                     # :254 → response.to_a → [status, headers, body]
end
```

`process(name)` is inherited from `AbstractController::Base`. It calls `send(name)` (after action callbacks from `ActionController::Callbacks`), which is the actual controller action method — `show`, `index`, etc.

`to_a` (`metal.rb:280`) returns `response.to_a`, which is `[status_code, headers_hash, body_array]` — the Rack triple that travels back up the stack.

---

## Unified Call Graph

```
═══════════════ DECLARATION PHASE (boot) ═══════════════

RouteSet#draw                            route_set.rb:457
 └─ RouteSet#eval_block                  route_set.rb:472
     └─ Mapper.new(route_set)            route_set.rb:473
         └─ Mapper#instance_exec(&block)
             │
             ├─ Mapper#get / #post / ...  mapper.rb:738
             │   └─ Mapper#match          mapper.rb:1837
             │       └─ Mapper#map_match  mapper.rb:2131
             │           └─ Mapper#decomposed_match   mapper.rb:2180
             │               └─ Mapper#add_route [private]   mapper.rb:2195
             │                   ├─ Mapping.normalize_path   mapper.rb:2210
             │                   ├─ Journey::Parser.parse    mapper.rb:2211  → AST nodes
             │                   ├─ Mapping.build            mapper.rb:2213
             │                   │   └─ Mapping#initialize   mapper.rb:132
             │                   │       └─ Journey::Path::Pattern.new  mapper.rb:178
             │                   └─ RouteSet#add_route       route_set.rb:643
             │                       └─ Journey::Routes#add_route  routes.rb:67
             │                           ├─ Mapping#make_route      routes.rb:68
             │                           │   └─ Journey::Route.new  route.rb:79
             │                           ├─ routes << route          routes.rb:69
             │                           ├─ Routes#partition_route   routes.rb:70
             │                           └─ Routes#clear_cache!      routes.rb:71
             │
             └─ Mapper#resources          mapper.rb:1663
                 └─ (calls get/post/patch/put/delete for 7 standard actions)
                    └─ (same chain as above)

[lazy on first request]
Journey::Routes#simulator               routes.rb:60
 ├─ GTG::Builder.new(ast).transition_table   gtg/builder.rb:21
 └─ GTG::Simulator.new(transition_table)     gtg/simulator.rb:27

═══════════════ DISPATCH PHASE (per request) ═══════════════

Rack env
 └─ RouteSet#call                        route_set.rb:903
     ├─ ActionDispatch::Request.new(env) route_set.rb:904
     ├─ Journey::Router::Utils.normalize_path  route_set.rb:905
     └─ Journey::Router#serve            router.rb:30
         └─ Journey::Router#recognize    router.rb:43
             ├─ Router#filter_routes     router.rb:48, 111
             │   └─ GTG::Simulator#memos  gtg/simulator.rb:31
             │       ├─ tt.move(state, string, token, ...)  [per byte]  simulator.rb:42,49
             │       ├─ tt.accepting?(s) + tt.memo(s)       simulator.rb:59-60
             │       └─ → [Journey::Route, ...]  (anchored candidates)
             ├─ custom_routes path.match?(path_info)        router.rb:50-52
             ├─ Router#match_head_routes (HEAD fallback)    router.rb:115
             ├─ routes.select! { r.matches?(req) }          router.rb:57
             │   └─ Journey::Route#matches?                 route.rb:169
             │       ├─ @request_method_match.call(req)     route.rb:170  (VerbMatchers::GET etc.)
             │       └─ constraints.all? { value === req.send(method) }  route.rb:171
             ├─ routes.sort! by precedence                  router.rb:61
             └─ for each route:
                 ├─ r.path.match(path_info)                router.rb:67  (named captures)
```

---

## Action Dispatch Routing: End-to-End

There are two distinct phases: **declaration** (at boot) and **dispatch** (per request). They share the same data structures but run independently.

---

## Phase 1 — Route Declaration

### Entry point: `config/routes.rb`

```ruby
Rails.application.routes.draw do
  resources :posts
  get "photos/:id", to: "photos#show"
end
```

### `RouteSet#draw` — `actionpack/lib/action_dispatch/routing/route_set.rb:457`

```ruby
def draw(&block)
  clear! unless @disable_clear_and_finalize   # wipes @set (Journey::Routes)
  eval_block(block)                            # runs the DSL block
  finalize! unless @disable_clear_and_finalize # appends any deferred blocks
  nil
end
```

### `RouteSet#eval_block` — `route_set.rb:472`

```ruby
def eval_block(block)
  mapper = Mapper.new(self)                    # Mapper holds a reference to the RouteSet (@set)
  mapper.instance_exec(&block)                 # executes config/routes.rb in Mapper's context
end
```

`RouteSet#initialize` (`route_set.rb:386`) wires up three collaborators:

```
@set       = Journey::Routes.new   # the flat list of compiled routes
@router    = Journey::Router.new(@set)
@formatter = Journey::Formatter.new(self)
```

---

### The Mapper DSL — `actionpack/lib/action_dispatch/routing/mapper.rb`

`Mapper` (`mapper.rb:14`) is the object on which the block is `instance_exec`'d. Its methods are organised into four nested modules: `Base`, `HttpHelpers`, `Scoping`, `Resources`.

#### HTTP verb helpers — `mapper.rb:738`

```ruby
def get(*path_or_actions, ...)
  match(*path_or_actions, ..., via: :get)  # mapper.rb:754
end
# post:738, patch:786, put:~822, delete:~856 — all identical shape
```

All delegate immediately to `match`.

#### `Mapper#match` — `mapper.rb:1837`

Unpacks arguments, then for each path calls either directly or via a `defaults` wrapper:

```ruby
map_match(path_or_action, as:, via:, to:, ...)   # mapper.rb:1885
```

#### `Mapper#map_match` — `mapper.rb:2131` (private)

Resolves controller/action from scope stack, validates `via:` via `Mapping.check_via` (`mapper.rb:2145`), then calls:

```ruby
decomposed_match(path, controller, as, action, ...)   # mapper.rb:2157
```

#### `Mapper#decomposed_match` — `mapper.rb:2180` (private)

Handles `on: :member/:collection` nesting and `:resources`/`:resource` scope levels by re-entering itself recursively. In the plain case:

```ruby
add_route(path, controller, as, action, ...)   # mapper.rb:2190
```

#### `Mapper#add_route` — `mapper.rb:2195` (private) — **the heart of registration**

```ruby
def add_route(action, controller, as, ...)
  path     = path_for_action(action, _path)                   # builds the full path string
  path     = Mapping.normalize_path(URI::RFC2396_PARSER.escape(path), formatted)  # :2210
  ast      = Journey::Parser.parse(path)                      # :2211 — string → AST
  mapping  = Mapping.build(@scope, @set, ast, controller, ...) # :2213
  @set.add_route(mapping, as)                                 # :2214 → RouteSet#add_route
end
```

#### `Journey::Parser.parse` — `actionpack/lib/action_dispatch/journey/parser.rb`

Converts the path string (`"/photos/:id(.:format)"`) into a tree of `Journey::Nodes` — `Literal`, `Symbol`, `Group`, `Star`, `Slash`, `Dot`, `Cat`, `Or`. This AST is what everything downstream compiles.

#### `Mapping.build` / `Mapping#initialize` — `mapper.rb:90` / `mapper.rb:132`

`Mapping` is a value object that encapsulates everything about one route. During `initialize`:

- merges scope defaults, constraints, module prefix
- calls `Journey::Ast.new(ast, formatted)` to annotate the AST
- computes `@requirements`, `@conditions`, `@defaults`
- builds the compiled pattern: `Journey::Path::Pattern.new(ast, @requirements, JOINED_SEPARATORS, @anchor)` — `mapper.rb:178`

`Mapping#make_route` (`mapper.rb:183`) creates the actual `Journey::Route`:

```ruby
def make_route(name, precedence)
  Journey::Route.new(
    name: name,
    app: application,          # Dispatcher or Constraints wrapper
    path: path,                # Journey::Path::Pattern
    constraints: conditions,
    defaults: defaults,
    via: @via,
    precedence: precedence,
    ...
  )
end
```

`application` (`mapper.rb:190`) builds the endpoint: if there are constraint blocks, it wraps `Dispatcher.new` in `Constraints.new(dispatcher, blocks, SERVE)`.

---

### `RouteSet#add_route` — `route_set.rb:643`

```ruby
def add_route(mapping, name)
  # validates name uniqueness
  route = @set.add_route(name, mapping)   # :654 → Journey::Routes
  named_routes[name] = route if name      # registers helper (posts_path, posts_url)
  route
end
```

---

### `Journey::Routes#add_route` — `actionpack/lib/action_dispatch/journey/routes.rb:67`

```ruby
def add_route(name, mapping)
  route = mapping.make_route(name, routes.length)  # :68 → Journey::Route.new
  routes << route                                   # :69 — appended to flat list
  partition_route(route)                            # :70
  clear_cache!                                      # :71 — clears @ast and @simulator
  route
end
```

`partition_route` (`routes.rb:45`) splits routes into two buckets:

- **`anchored_routes`** — static-prefix routes whose path is fully anchored and requirements-anchored. These get compiled into the GTG automaton.
- **`custom_routes`** — dynamic/unanchored routes (e.g. mounted engines, `match "*path"`). Matched by regex at request time.

---

### `Journey::Route.new` — `actionpack/lib/action_dispatch/journey/route.rb:79`

Stores: `@name`, `@app` (the endpoint), `@path` (Pattern), `@request_method_match` (a `VerbMatchers::GET`, `Or`, `All`, etc.), `@constraints`, `@defaults`, `@precedence`.

`VerbMatchers` (`route.rb:14`) are tiny classes; `VerbMatchers.for(via)` (`route.rb:65`) selects or composes them.

---

### GTG Simulator: built lazily on first request

`Journey::Routes#simulator` (`routes.rb:60`):

```ruby
def simulator
  @simulator ||= begin
    gtg = GTG::Builder.new(ast).transition_table   # compiles NFA→DFA from the Or-of-all-anchored-routes AST
    GTG::Simulator.new(gtg)
  end
end
```

`GTG::Builder#transition_table` (`gtg/builder.rb:21`) runs the standard subset-construction algorithm over the AST's first-pos / follow-pos sets, producing a `TransitionTable` — a hash-of-hashes keyed by `(state, token)`.

---

## Phase 2 — Request Dispatch

Every request enters via the Rack interface.

### `RouteSet#call` — `route_set.rb:903`

```ruby
def call(env)
  req = make_request(env)                                      # ActionDispatch::Request.new(env)
  req.path_info = Journey::Router::Utils.normalize_path(req.path_info)  # strip trailing slash, etc.
  @router.serve(req)
end
```

---

### `Journey::Router#serve` — `actionpack/lib/action_dispatch/journey/router.rb:30`

```ruby
def serve(req)
  recognize(req) do |route, parameters|
    req.path_parameters = parameters   # merges into rack env
    req.route = route

    _, headers, _ = response = route.app.serve(req)   # :35 — calls endpoint

    return response unless headers[Constants::X_CASCADE] == "pass"
    # if endpoint passes (cascade), try next matched route
  end

  [404, { Constants::X_CASCADE => "pass" }, ["Not Found"]]
end
```

---

### `Journey::Router#recognize` — `router.rb:43`

```ruby
def recognize(req, &block)
  path_info = req.path_info

  routes = filter_routes(path_info)    # :48 — GTG fast path

  custom_routes.each { |r|             # :50 — regex fallback for unanchored routes
    routes << r if r.path.match?(path_info)
  }

  if req.head?
    routes = match_head_routes(routes, req)   # :55 — HEAD → try GET
  else
    routes.select! { |r| r.matches?(req) }   # :57 — verb + constraints filter
  end

  if routes.size > 1
    routes.sort! { |a, b| a.precedence <=> b.precedence }  # :61
  end

  routes.each do |r|
    match_data = r.path.match(path_info)           # :67 — Regexp named captures

    path_parameters = req_params.merge r.defaults  # :69

    match_data.names.each do |name|                # :72 — populate :id, :format, etc.
      val = match_data[index]
      val = CGI.unescapeURIComponent(val) if val.include?("%")  # :75
      path_parameters[name.to_sym] = val
    end

    yield(r, path_parameters)                      # :86 — back to serve's block
  end
end
```

#### `Journey::Router#filter_routes` — `router.rb:111` (private)

```ruby
def filter_routes(path)
  simulator.memos(path) { [] }
end
```

---

### `GTG::Simulator#memos` — `actionpack/lib/action_dispatch/journey/gtg/simulator.rb:31`

```ruby
def memos(string)
  state = INITIAL_STATE   # [0, nil]

  pos = 0
  while pos < string.bytesize
    # classify byte: ".", "/", "?" are STATIC_TOKENS; everything else is a dynamic token
    token = STATIC_TOKENS[string.getbyte(pos)] || read_dynamic_token(string, pos)
    state = tt.move(state, string, token, start_index, dynamic?)  # walk DFA
  end

  # collect all accepting DFA states
  acceptance_states = state.each_slice(2).filter_map { |s, _|
    tt.memo(s) if tt.accepting?(s)
  }.flatten

  acceptance_states.empty? ? yield : acceptance_states  # :66 — yield = [] (no match)
end
```

Each DFA accepting state carries a `memo` — the `Journey::Route` objects that terminate at that state. The return value is the list of candidate routes, not yet verb-filtered.

---

### `Journey::Route#matches?` — `route.rb:169`

Called by `router.rb:57` (`routes.select! { |r| r.matches?(req) }`):

```ruby
def matches?(request)
  @request_method_match.call(request) &&   # :170 — e.g. VerbMatchers::GET.call(req) → req.get?
  constraints.all? { |method, value|       # :171 — host, subdomain, custom constraints
    value === request.send(method).to_s
  }
end
```

---

### `route.app.serve(req)` — the endpoint chain

The `app` stored on a route is one of:

1. **`Mapper::Constraints`** (`mapper.rb:29`) — a wrapper that adds runtime constraint objects (lambdas, objects with `matches?`). Its `serve` (`mapper.rb:59`) checks `matches?` then delegates to its inner app.
2. **`RouteSet::Dispatcher`** (`route_set.rb:39`) — the standard controller dispatcher.
3. A raw Rack app (from `mount SomeEngine`).

### `RouteSet::Dispatcher#serve` — `route_set.rb:46`

```ruby
def serve(req)
  params     = req.path_parameters              # {:controller=>"photos", :action=>"show", :id=>"1"}
  controller = controller(req)                  # req.controller_class → Photos::PhotosController
  res        = controller.make_response!(req)   # ActionDispatch::Response
  dispatch(controller, params[:action], req, res)
end

def dispatch(controller, action, req, res)
  controller.dispatch(action, req, res)         # :65 → class-level dispatch
end
```

`req.controller_class` (`ActionDispatch::Request`) resolves `params[:controller]` to a constant — `"photos"` → `PhotosController`.

---

### `ActionController::Metal.dispatch` — `actionpack/lib/action_controller/metal.rb:331` (class method)

```ruby
def self.dispatch(name, req, res)
  if middleware_stack.any?
    middleware_stack.build(name) { |env| new.dispatch(name, req, res) }.call(req.env)  # :333
  else
    new.dispatch(name, req, res)   # :335
  end
end
```

If the controller has per-controller middleware (`use AuthMiddleware`), it is applied here.

---

### `ActionController::Metal#dispatch` — `metal.rb:249` (instance method)

```ruby
def dispatch(name, request, response)
  set_request!(request)    # :250 — @_request = request; request.controller_instance = self
  set_response!(response)  # :251
  process(name)            # :252 — AbstractController::Base#process → calls action method
  request.commit_flash     # :253
  to_a                     # :254 → response.to_a → [status, headers, body]
end
```

`process(name)` is inherited from `AbstractController::Base`. It calls `send(name)` (after action callbacks from `ActionController::Callbacks`), which is the actual controller action method — `show`, `index`, etc.

`to_a` (`metal.rb:280`) returns `response.to_a`, which is `[status_code, headers_hash, body_array]` — the Rack triple that travels back up the stack.
